message:
docs(readme): disclose ft snapshot restore Unix-only status (ft-6269x)

ft-2guux previously closed the `ft restart` Unix-only disclosure
gap by updating the README session-persistence matrix at line 128.
That fix stopped at `ft restart` but the same cfg gate applies to
`ft snapshot restore` — both surfaces go through identical
`#[cfg(not(unix))]` branches that emit "currently supported only on
Unix platforms." and exit 1:

  crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:27100  (ft restart)
  crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:39715  (ft snapshot restore)

Verified these are the only Unix-only exit points in main.rs —
grep -n 'currently supported only on Unix' returns exactly the two
sites.

## Fix

Rewrote the Session persistence matrix row to:

- State the cross-platform surfaces up front (snapshots, session
  inspection, `ft session doctor` — the observation/diagnostics half).
- Name BOTH Unix-only commands (`ft restart` and `ft snapshot
  restore`) so a Windows operator reading the matrix is not told
  "restore is implemented" without the caveat.
- Cite main.rs:27100 + :39715 so a future reader can verify the
  claim without grepping from scratch.

Pragmatic docs-first fix per the bead's recommendation. Porting the
gate would require teaching the session-restore pipeline to not
need Unix fork/signal semantics, which is a separate lane tracked
under the session-persistence epic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -125,7 +125,7 @@ The project needs an honest status table, not migration-era hand-waving.
 | Watch / status / triage / doctor / reproduce | Supported | Native operator surfaces; `ft doctor`, `ft status --health`, and `ft triage` are the first-run and incident entrypoints. |
 | Search / events / audit / workflows / mission / tx | Supported | Backed by local storage, policy, and workflow subsystems. |
 | Robot mode | Supported | Core families are implemented: state, get-text, send, wait-for, search, events, rules, workflows, agents, accounts, reservations, mission, tx, health, approvals, and the NTM-aligned families listed by `ft robot help` (or `ft robot --format toon help` for compact machine output). |
-| Session persistence | Supported with backend prerequisite | Snapshots, session inspection, restore, and `ft session doctor` are implemented, but live restore still depends on the current WezTerm mux interop boundary. `ft restart` is currently Unix-only. |
+| Session persistence | Supported with backend prerequisite | Snapshots, session inspection, and `ft session doctor` are implemented cross-platform; live restore still depends on the current WezTerm mux interop boundary. `ft restart` and `ft snapshot restore` are both currently Unix-only (`#[cfg(not(unix))]` emits "currently supported only on Unix platforms" and exits 1 — see main.rs:27100 + :39715). |
 | Web API / SSE | Supported behind `--features web` | `/health`, `/panes`, `/events`, `/search`, and `/stream/*` are implemented; this is more than "health only". |
 | Distributed mode | Supported with explicit limitations | Remote panes persist into the same DB and surface through status/search/state, but live `get-text` for distributed panes is intentionally unavailable. |
 | Semantic search | Optional | Enabled only when built/configured for embeddings. |
